reference_answer: McDonnell Douglas MD-11F (also described as Boeing (McDonnell-Douglas) MD-11F), registration N259UP.

reference_answer: Visual meteorological conditions (VMC), day; scattered clouds at 20,000 ft AGL; broken ceiling at 25,000 ft AGL; visibility 10 miles; wind 6 knots from 120 degrees (no gusts); temperature 17 C, dew point 5 C; altimeter setting 30.22 inches Hg.
supporting_source_spans: Page 12 Meteorological Information and Flight Plan table

reference_answer: GE CF-6 series engines.
supporting_source_spans: Page 5 Airplane and Operator Information: 'The accident airplane was equipped with GE CF-6 engines.'

reference_answer: DCA26MA024.

reference_answer: Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), UPS, The Boeing Company, Independent Pilots Association (IPA), General Electric (GE) Aerospace, and Teamsters Airline Division.

reference_answer: Shin, Chihoon.
supporting_source_spans: Page 12 Administrative Information: 'Investigator In Charge (IIC): Shin, Chihoon'

reference_answer: The accident occurred at about 17:14 EST (1714 local) on November 4, 2025. UPS flight 2976 received takeoff clearance from SDF ATC tower at about 1711 EST.
supporting_source_spans: Page 1 header: 'Date & Time: November 4, 2025, 17:14 Local'
Page 1 History of Flight: 'received their takeoff clearance from SDF ATC tower about 1711 and the flight crew acknowledged the takeoff clearance'

reference_answer: The flight data started about 1707 EST and ended about 1713:30 EST. The takeoff roll began about 1712 EST.
supporting_source_spans: Page 5 Recorders: 'The flight data for the accident flight started about 1707 EST, with the takeoff roll starting about 1712 EST. The flight data ended about 1713:30 EST.'

reference_answer: Emergency AD 2025-23-51 was issued by the FAA on November 8, 2025. Emergency AD 2025-23-53 was issued on November 14, 2025 and superseded Emergency AD 2025-23-51; the later AD also expanded scope to include DC-10 series airplanes based on their similar design to the MD-11.
supporting_source_spans: Pages 10-11 Safety Actions section

reference_answer: November 5, 2025.
supporting_source_spans: Page 1: 'In response to the accident, the NTSB traveled to SDF on November 5, 2025'

reference_answer: 14 fatal, 2 serious, and 21 minor injuries. Crew: 3 fatal (all 3 crewmembers aboard). Ground: 11 fatal, 2 serious, 21 minor.
supporting_source_spans: Page 1 header injury summary
Page 12 Wreckage and Impact Information table

reference_answer: About 92,992 total hours and 21,043 cycles.
supporting_source_spans: Page 10 Maintenance and Inspections: 'N259UP had accumulated a total time of about 92,992 hours and 21,043 cycles'

reference_answer: About 450 parameters; about 63 hours of FDR data spanning 24 flights, including the accident flight.
supporting_source_spans: Page 5 Recorders section

reference_answer: About 30 ft above ground level (agl).
supporting_source_spans: Page 2 History of Flight: 'The airplane initially climbed but did not get higher than about 30 ft above ground level (agl) according to radio altitude data from the FDR.'

reference_answer: A witness in the SDF ATC tower.
supporting_source_spans: Page 4 History of Flight: 'A witness in the SDF ATC tower reported that the takeoff speed appeared normal for that type of aircraft; however, the climb rate was not normal, as the airplane did not climb above the tower's height of approximately 200 feet agl.'

reference_answer: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) issued Emergency AD 2025-23-51. Corrective actions had to be performed using a method approved by the FAA Continued Operational Safety Branch (AIR-520).
supporting_source_spans: Page 10 Safety Actions section

reference_answer: At the recommendation of Boeing. UPS grounded its MD-11 fleet on November 7, 2025 as a precautionary measure and at Boeing's recommendation.
supporting_source_spans: Page 10 Safety Actions section

reference_answer: FDR radio altitude data indicates the airplane did not get higher than about 30 ft agl. FAA-provided ADS-B data, however, shows the last data point at 481 ft mean sea level (msl) and 100 ft agl. The report does not reconcile the two values.
supporting_source_spans: Page 2 History of Flight: 'about 30 ft above ground level (agl) according to radio altitude data from the FDR. (Based on FAA-provided ADS-B data, the last data point showed 481 ft mean sea level [msl] and 100 ft agl.)'

reference_answer: SDI of the left pylon aft mount lugs would have been due at 29,200 cycles; SDI of the left wing clevis support would have been due at 28,000 cycles. The accident airplane had accumulated 21,043 cycles. The report notes both SDI tasks had not been accomplished, but the airplane had not yet reached either threshold.
supporting_source_spans: Page 10 Maintenance and Inspections: SDI cycles for left pylon aft mount lugs (29,200) and left wing clevis support (28,000); airplane cycles 21,043

reference_answer: An Air Traffic Controller (ATC) group was not formed, even though an NTSB ATC specialist was on site to collect ATC information.
supporting_source_spans: Page 1: 'A NTSB Air Traffic Controller (ATC) specialist was on site to collect ATC information, but an ATC group was not formed.'

reference_answer: No. The preliminary report does not state a probable cause. Every page is labeled 'This information is preliminary and subject to change,' and the report ends 'The NTSB's investigation of UPS flight 2976 is ongoing.'
supporting_source_spans: Recurring page footer 'This information is preliminary and subject to change'
Page 11 closing line: 'The NTSB's investigation of UPS flight 2976 is ongoing.'

reference_answer: The Passenger Injuries field in the Wreckage and Impact Information table is blank/not stated. The flight was a domestic cargo flight under Part 121 with only 3 crewmembers aboard; the 11 fatal, 2 serious, and 21 minor injuries reported are all ground injuries.
supporting_source_spans: Page 12 Wreckage and Impact Information table: Passenger Injuries field blank

reference_answer: Both accidents involved separation of the left engine and pylon assembly from the left wing during or shortly after takeoff rotation. For UPS 2976 (Nov 4, 2025), the airplane initially climbed but did not get higher than about 30 ft agl per FDR radio altitude data, then stopped climbing and began to lose altitude before rolling slightly to the left. For American Airlines flight 191 (May 25, 1979, DC-10-10), the airplane continued to climb to about 325 ft agl, then began to roll to the left until the wings were past the vertical position, with the nose pitching down below the horizon.
supporting_source_spans: Page 2 History of Flight (UPS 2976)
Page 11 Similar Events (AA 191, May 25, 1979, DCA79AA017 / AAR-79-17)
